title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Total member shares stood at $845.47 million as of December 31, 2026, representing a 1.2% increase from the previous year-end. The growth reflects steady accumulation across all core deposit categories amid continued balance-sheet adjustments and member funding inflows. Despite minor fluctuations in specific segments, the funding base remains predominantly member-driven and continues to provide stable support for the credit union's balance sheet.

Share drafts remain the largest funding component at 29.9% of total shares, totaling $253.06 million. Share certificates account for $254.83 million (30.1%), while regular shares total $208.69 million (24.7%). Money market shares represent $104.46 million (12.4%) of the funding mix, continuing to serve as a meaningful portion of shorter-term member deposits. IRA/KEOGH accounts total $23.88 million (2.8%), while all other share categories and nonmember deposits account for minimal portions at 0.1% and 0.0%, respectively.

Most core deposit categories experienced growth from the previous year-end, particularly money market shares (+0.2%), regular shares (+3.2%), share certificates (+0.2%), and share drafts (+1.2%). All other shares increased by a notable 6.9% during the period, while nonmember deposits held flat at -0.0%. The growth in all major categories underscores strong member engagement and effective deposit retention strategies.

Overall, Campus's deposit structure remains well diversified and predominantly composed of core member funding. While total shares grew modestly during the period, the institution maintains a stable funding base and robust liquidity position. Management continues to focus on retaining core member relationships, maintaining deposit competitiveness, and supporting long-term funding stability.
